subject: Chemistry
text: Geometry Pyramidal shape
text_html: Geometry Pyramidal shape

subject: Chemistry
text: Amines as Derivatives of Ammonia Amines are organic compounds derived from ammonia by replacing one or more hydrogen atoms with alkyl or aryl groups. Primary Amines: One hydrogen replaced Secondary Amines: Two hydrogens replaced Tertiary Amines: Three hydrogens replaced
text_html:
Amines as Derivatives of Ammonia
Amines are organic compounds derived from ammonia by replacing one or more hydrogen atoms with alkyl or aryl groups.
  • Primary Amines: One hydrogen replaced
  • Secondary Amines: Two hydrogens replaced
  • Tertiary Amines: Three hydrogens replaced

subject: Chemistry
text: Natural Occurrence of Amines Amines are found naturally in several biological compounds, including: Proteins Vitamins Alkaloids Hormones
text_html:
Natural Occurrence of Amines
Amines are found naturally in several biological compounds, including:
  • Proteins
  • Vitamins
  • Alkaloids
  • Hormones

subject: Chemistry
text: Synthetic Examples of Amines Examples of synthetic amines include: Polymers Dye stuffs Drugs
text_html:
Synthetic Examples of Amines
Examples of synthetic amines include:
  • Polymers
  • Dye stuffs
  • Drugs

subject: Chemistry
text: Biologically Active Amines Important compounds containing secondary amino groups: Adrenaline: Increases blood pressure Ephedrine: Similarly used to increase blood pressure
text_html:
Biologically Active Amines
Important compounds containing secondary amino groups:
  • Adrenaline: Increases blood pressure
  • Ephedrine: Similarly used to increase blood pressure

subject: Chemistry
text: Applications of Amines in Medicine Medicinal uses of amines: Novocain: Anesthetic used in dentistry Benadryl: Antihistaminic drug containing a tertiary amino group
text_html:
Applications of Amines in Medicine
Medicinal uses of amines:
  • Novocain: Anesthetic used in dentistry
  • Benadryl: Antihistaminic drug containing a tertiary amino group

subject: Chemistry
text: Surfactants and Amines Quaternary ammonium salts, a type of amine, are commonly used as surfactants.
text_html:
Surfactants and Amines
Quaternary ammonium salts, a type of amine, are commonly used as surfactants.

subject: Chemistry
text: Role of Diazonium Salts Diazonium salts serve as intermediates in preparing aromatic compounds, including a variety of dyes.
text_html:
Role of Diazonium Salts
Diazonium salts serve as intermediates in preparing aromatic compounds, including a variety of dyes.

subject: Chemistry
text: Structure of Amines Amines have a pyramidal geometry due to the sp3 hybridization of the nitrogen atom, which carries an unshared pair of electrons.
text_html:
Structure of Amines
Amines have a pyramidal geometry due to the sp3 hybridization of the nitrogen atom, which carries an unshared pair of electrons.

subject: Chemistry
text: Geometry of Amines The geometry of amines is determined by the sp3 hybridization of nitrogen, resulting in a pyramidal shape with a bond angle less than $$ 109.5^{\circ} $$.
text_html:
Geometry of Amines
The geometry of amines is determined by the sp3 hybridization of nitrogen, resulting in a pyramidal shape with a bond angle less than $$ 109.5^{\circ} $$.

subject: Chemistry
text: Hybridization in Amines Nitrogen in amines undergoes sp3 hybridization, involving overlap with hydrogen or carbon orbitals, and has a lone pair of electrons.
text_html:
Hybridization in Amines
Nitrogen in amines undergoes sp3 hybridization, involving overlap with hydrogen or carbon orbitals, and has a lone pair of electrons.

subject: Chemistry
text: Classification
text_html: Classification

subject: Chemistry
text: Amines Classification Amines are categorized into primary, secondary, and tertiary based on hydrogen atoms replaced by alkyl or aryl groups in ammonia.
text_html:
Amines Classification
Amines are categorized into primary, secondary, and tertiary based on hydrogen atoms replaced by alkyl or aryl groups in ammonia.

subject: Chemistry
text: Primary Amines (1 o ) In primary amines, one hydrogen atom of ammonia is replaced by an R (alkyl) or Ar (aryl) group, forming RNH 2 or ArNH 2 .
text_html:
Primary Amines (1o)
In primary amines, one hydrogen atom of ammonia is replaced by an R (alkyl) or Ar (aryl) group, forming RNH2 or ArNH2.

subject: Chemistry
text: Secondary Amines (2 o ) Secondary amines are formed when two hydrogens are replaced in ammonia or one in a primary amine, resulting in R-NHR'. This involves two R or Ar groups.
text_html:
Secondary Amines (2o)
Secondary amines are formed when two hydrogens are replaced in ammonia or one in a primary amine, resulting in R-NHR'. This involves two R or Ar groups.

subject: Chemistry
text: Tertiary Amines (3 o ) Tertiary amines form when a third hydrogen in ammonia is replaced, resulting in a structure where all three hydrogens are substituted by R or Ar.
text_html:
Tertiary Amines (3o)
Tertiary amines form when a third hydrogen in ammonia is replaced, resulting in a structure where all three hydrogens are substituted by R or Ar.

subject: Chemistry
text: Simple vs. Mixed Amines Amines are termed 'simple' if all substituent groups (R/Ar) are the same, and 'mixed' if they are different.
text_html:
Simple vs. Mixed Amines
Amines are termed 'simple' if all substituent groups (R/Ar) are the same, and 'mixed' if they are different.

subject: Chemistry
text: Representation of Primary Amines One R group: RNH 2 Example: Methylamine (CH 3 NH 2 )
text_html:
Representation of Primary Amines
  • One R group: RNH2
  • Example: Methylamine (CH3NH2)

subject: Chemistry
text: Illustration of Secondary Amines Secondary aminStructure is R-NHR'. Replace hydrogen in a primary amine to form secondary amines, e.g., Dimethylamine (CH 3 NHCH 3 ).
text_html:
Illustration of Secondary Amines
Secondary aminStructure is R-NHR'. Replace hydrogen in a primary amine to form secondary amines, e.g., Dimethylamine (CH3NHCH3).

subject: Chemistry
text: Understanding Tertiary Amines Three R groups Example tertiary amines: Trimethylamine ((CH 3 ) 3 N)
text_html:
Understanding Tertiary Amines
  • Three R groups
  • Example tertiary amines: Trimethylamine ((CH3)3N)

subject: Chemistry
text: Factors Influencing Amines The type of amines depends on the number and type of substituent R or Ar groups attached to the nitrogen.
text_html:
Factors Influencing Amines
The type of amines depends on the number and type of substituent R or Ar groups attached to the nitrogen.

subject: Chemistry
text: Quiz Yourself Can you identify which type of amine forms from replacing another hydrogen in a secondary amine? Structure of mixed tertiary amine?
text_html:
Quiz Yourself
  • Can you identify which type of amine forms from replacing another hydrogen in a secondary amine?
  • Structure of mixed tertiary amine?

subject: Chemistry
text: Nomenclature Common Names:- Amine is used as suffix after alkyl group e.g. CH 3 CH 2 NH 2 is ethylamine IUPAC names: e is replaced by - amine e.g. CH 3 CH 2 NH 2 is ethanamine.
text_html:
Nomenclature
Common Names:- Amine is used as suffix after alkyl group e.g. CH3CH2NH2 is ethylamine
IUPAC names: e is replaced by - amine e.g. CH3CH2NH2 is ethanamine.

subject: Chemistry
text: Basic Naming of Aliphatic Amines An aliphatic amine is named by prefixing the alkyl group to the term 'amine.' For instance, when an amine is derived from methane, it is called 'methylamine.'
text_html:
Basic Naming of Aliphatic Amines
An aliphatic amine is named by prefixing the alkyl group to the term 'amine.' For instance, when an amine is derived from methane, it is called 'methylamine.'

subject: Chemistry
text: Secondary and Tertiary Amines Naming When naming secondary and tertiary amines with identical groups, prefixes such as 'di' or 'tri' are added before the alkyl group. E.g., dimethylamine, trimethylamine.
text_html:
Secondary and Tertiary Amines Naming
When naming secondary and tertiary amines with identical groups, prefixes such as 'di' or 'tri' are added before the alkyl group. E.g., dimethylamine, trimethylamine.

subject: Chemistry
text: IUPAC System for Primary Amines Primary amines in the IUPAC system are called alkanamines. For example, {tex} {CH}_3{NH}_2{/tex} is methanamine, replacing the final 'e' of alkane with 'amine.'
text_html:
IUPAC System for Primary Amines
Primary amines in the IUPAC system are called alkanamines. For example, {tex} {CH}_3{NH}_2{/tex} is methanamine, replacing the final 'e' of alkane with 'amine.'

subject: Chemistry
text: IUPAC Naming Multiple Amino Groups When more than one amino group is present, numbers denote their positions, and suitable prefixes like 'di' or 'tri' are added. For instance, {tex} {H}_2 {~N}-{CH}_2-{CH}_2-{NH}_2 {/tex} is ethane-1, 2-diamine.
text_html:
IUPAC Naming Multiple Amino Groups
When more than one amino group is present, numbers denote their positions, and suitable prefixes like 'di' or 'tri' are added. For instance, {tex} {H}_2 {~N}-{CH}_2-{CH}_2-{NH}_2 {/tex} is ethane-1, 2-diamine.

subject: Chemistry
text: Naming Secondary and Tertiary Amines in IUPAC Use the locant N to designate substituents attached to a nitrogen atom. E.g., {tex} {CH}_3 {NHCH}_2 {CH}_3 {/tex} becomes N-methylethanamine.
text_html:
Naming Secondary and Tertiary Amines in IUPAC
Use the locant N to designate substituents attached to a nitrogen atom. E.g., {tex} {CH}_3 {NHCH}_2 {CH}_3 {/tex} becomes N-methylethanamine.

subject: Chemistry
text: Multiple Substituents on Nitrogen For tertiary amines with multiple similar groups, use N, N-dialkylamine format. E.g., {tex} \left({CH}_3{CH}_2 \right)_3{~N} {/tex} translates to {tex}{/tex} {N},{N}-diethylethanamine.
text_html:
Multiple Substituents on Nitrogen
For tertiary amines with multiple similar groups, use N, N-dialkylamine format. E.g., {tex} \left({CH}_3{CH}_2 \right)_3{~N} {/tex} translates to {tex}{/tex} {N},{N}-diethylethanamine.

subject: Chemistry
text: Arylamines Structure The {tex} - {NH}_2 {/tex} group in arylamines is attached directly to a benzene ring. The simplest arylamine is {tex} {C}_6{H}_5{NH}_2{/tex} , known as aniline.
text_html:
Arylamines Structure
The {tex} - {NH}_2 {/tex} group in arylamines is attached directly to a benzene ring. The simplest arylamine is {tex} {C}_6{H}_5{NH}_2{/tex}, known as aniline.

subject: Chemistry
text: IUPAC Naming of Arylamines In IUPAC nomenclature, arylamines swap the 'e' of arene with 'amine.' For instance, benzenamine is the IUPAC name for {tex} {C}_6{H}_5{NH}_2{/tex} .
text_html:
IUPAC Naming of Arylamines
In IUPAC nomenclature, arylamines swap the 'e' of arene with 'amine.' For instance, benzenamine is the IUPAC name for {tex} {C}_6{H}_5{NH}_2{/tex}.

subject: Chemistry
text: Common vs. IUPAC Names Aniline is both the common and IUPAC name. Anilist of additional examples can be found in a table (Table 9.1), showcasing further differentiation.
text_html:
Common vs. IUPAC Names
  • Aniline is both the common and IUPAC name.
  • Anilist of additional examples can be found in a table (Table 9.1), showcasing further differentiation.

subject: Chemistry
text: Table Reference The table 9.1 provides various examples of both common and IUPAC names for alkylamines and arylamines, illustrating the differences and applications of the discussed naming conventions.
text_html:
Table Reference
The table 9.1 provides various examples of both common and IUPAC names for alkylamines and arylamines, illustrating the differences and applications of the discussed naming conventions.

text: Method of Preparation: Reduction of Nitro compounds
text_html:
Method of Preparation:
Reduction of Nitro compounds

subject: Chemistry
text: Method of Preparation: Ammonolysis Alkyl Halides
text_html:
Method of Preparation:
Ammonolysis Alkyl Halides

subject: Chemistry
text: Method of Preparation: Reduction of nitriles
text_html:
Method of Preparation:
Reduction of nitriles

subject: Chemistry
text: Method of Preparation: Reduction of amide {tex}R-\overset O{\overset{\vert\vert}C}-NH_2\xrightarrow[{H_2O}]{LiAlH_4}R-CH_2NH_2{/tex}
text_html:
Method of Preparation:
Reduction of amide {tex}R-\overset O{\overset{\vert\vert}C}-NH_2\xrightarrow[{H_2O}]{LiAlH_4}R-CH_2NH_2{/tex}

subject: Chemistry
text: Method of Preparation: Gabriel phthalimide sythesis
text_html:
Method of Preparation:
Gabriel phthalimide sythesis

subject: Chemistry
text: Method of Preparation: Hoffmann bromamide degeneration reaction. + Br 2 + 4NaOH {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} R-NH 2 + Na 2 CO 3 + 2NaBr + H 2 O
text_html:
Method of Preparation:
Hoffmann bromamide degeneration reaction.
 + Br2 + 4NaOH {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} R-NH2 + Na2CO3 + 2NaBr + H2O

subject: Chemistry
text: Reduction of Nitro Compounds Nitro compounds are transformed into amines by reduction using hydrogen gas with nickel, palladium, or platinum catalysts. Metal reduction in an acidic medium is also a method, with iron scrap and hydrochloric acid being preferred. FeCl2 hydrolyses to regenerate HCl. Hydrochloric acid initiates the reaction with minimal requirement.
text_html:
Reduction of Nitro Compounds
Nitro compounds are transformed into amines by reduction using hydrogen gas with nickel, palladium, or platinum catalysts.
Metal reduction in an acidic medium is also a method, with iron scrap and hydrochloric acid being preferred.
  • FeCl2 hydrolyses to regenerate HCl.
  • Hydrochloric acid initiates the reaction with minimal requirement.

subject: Chemistry
text: Ammonolysis of Alkyl Halides Alkyl or benzyl halides react with ammonia to form an amine and release a halogen atom: The reaction is conducted in a sealed tube at 373 K. Primary amine is major when excess ammonia is used. Nucleophilic substitution reaction: Ammonia replaces halogen with an -NH2 group.
text_html:
Ammonolysis of Alkyl Halides
Alkyl or benzyl halides react with ammonia to form an amine and release a halogen atom:
  • The reaction is conducted in a sealed tube at 373 K.
  • Primary amine is major when excess ammonia is used.
Nucleophilic substitution reaction: Ammonia replaces halogen with an -NH2 group.

subject: Chemistry
text: Reduction of Nitriles Nitriles can be reduced with lithium aluminum hydride (LiAlH4) or by catalytic hydrogenation. This process makes primary amines with one more carbon atom than starting materials. Known as "ascent of amine series."
text_html:
Reduction of Nitriles
Nitriles can be reduced with lithium aluminum hydride (LiAlH4) or by catalytic hydrogenation.
  • This process makes primary amines with one more carbon atom than starting materials.
  • Known as "ascent of amine series."

subject: Chemistry
text: Reduction of Amides Amides are reduced by lithium aluminum hydride to form amines. This method is commonly employed in preparations requiring delicate conditions.
text_html:
Reduction of Amides
Amides are reduced by lithium aluminum hydride to form amines.
This method is commonly employed in preparations requiring delicate conditions.

subject: Chemistry
text: Gabriel Phthalimide Synthesis A synthesis method specifically for primary amines: Phthalimide turns into its potassium salt with ethanolic potassium hydroxide. On reacting with alkyl halide and undergoing alkaline hydrolysis, a primary amine forms. Aromatic primary amines are not produced due to resistance of aryl halides.
text_html:
Gabriel Phthalimide Synthesis
A synthesis method specifically for primary amines:
  • Phthalimide turns into its potassium salt with ethanolic potassium hydroxide.
  • On reacting with alkyl halide and undergoing alkaline hydrolysis, a primary amine forms.
  • Aromatic primary amines are not produced due to resistance of aryl halides.

subject: Chemistry
text: Hoffmann Bromamide Degradation Reaction Developed by Hoffmann for primary amine production: Treat amides with bromine and sodium hydroxide in aqueous or ethanolic solution. Alkyl or aryl group migrates from carbonyl carbon of amide to nitrogen. Amines formed contain one carbon less than amides.
text_html:
Hoffmann Bromamide Degradation Reaction
Developed by Hoffmann for primary amine production:
  • Treat amides with bromine and sodium hydroxide in aqueous or ethanolic solution.
  • Alkyl or aryl group migrates from carbonyl carbon of amide to nitrogen.
  • Amines formed contain one carbon less than amides.

subject: Chemistry
text: Ammonolysis Mixture Disadvantage Ammonolysis tends to yield a mixture: A mix of primary, secondary, tertiary amines and quaternary ammonium salt occurs. The major drawback is difficulty in controlling the proportion of products.
text_html:
Ammonolysis Mixture Disadvantage
Ammonolysis tends to yield a mixture:
  • A mix of primary, secondary, tertiary amines and quaternary ammonium salt occurs.
  • The major drawback is difficulty in controlling the proportion of products.

subject: Chemistry
text: Halide Reactivity with Amines The order of reactivity for halides is: RI > RBr > RCl. It affects how quickly and efficiently amines can be produced from various halides.
text_html:
Halide Reactivity with Amines
  • The order of reactivity for halides is: RI > RBr > RCl.
  • It affects how quickly and efficiently amines can be produced from various halides.

subject: Chemistry
text: Sealed Tube Reaction Condition For effective ammonolysis: Conduct reactions in a sealed environment (373 K) for optimal results. This condition ensures control and proper reaction progress.
text_html:
Sealed Tube Reaction Condition
For effective ammonolysis:
  • Conduct reactions in a sealed environment (373 K) for optimal results.
  • This condition ensures control and proper reaction progress.

subject: Chemistry
text: Avoidance of Aromatic Primary Amines in Gabriel Synthesis Aromatic primary amines cannot be synthesized via this route: Aryl halides exhibit resistance to nucleophilic substitution necessary for reaction completion.
text_html:
Avoidance of Aromatic Primary Amines in Gabriel Synthesis
Aromatic primary amines cannot be synthesized via this route:
  • Aryl halides exhibit resistance to nucleophilic substitution necessary for reaction completion.

subject: Chemistry
text: Aliphatic Amines Odour Lower aliphatic amines are gases with a distinct fishy odor. Higher aliphatic amines vary in state: primary amines with three or more carbon are liquid, and even higher ones are solid.
text_html:
Aliphatic Amines Odour
Lower aliphatic amines are gases with a distinct fishy odor. Higher aliphatic amines vary in state: primary amines with three or more carbon are liquid, and even higher ones are solid.

subject: Chemistry
text: Aryl Amines Characteristics Aniline and other arylamines are typically colorless when fresh but may become colored over time due to atmospheric oxidation.
text_html:
Aryl Amines Characteristics
Aniline and other arylamines are typically colorless when fresh but may become colored over time due to atmospheric oxidation.

subject: Chemistry
text: Solubility of Amines - Lower aliphatic amines are soluble in water due to hydrogen bonding. - Solubility diminishes as molar mass increases because of the growing hydrophobic alkyl group. - Higher amines are insoluble in water. - Amines dissolve well in organic solvents like alcohol, ether, and benzene.
text_html:
Solubility of Amines
- Lower aliphatic amines are soluble in water due to hydrogen bonding.
- Solubility diminishes as molar mass increases because of the growing hydrophobic alkyl group.
- Higher amines are insoluble in water.
- Amines dissolve well in organic solvents like alcohol, ether, and benzene.

subject: Chemistry
text: Comparative Solubility with Alcohols The electronegativity of nitrogen and oxygen are 3.0 and 3.5, respectively. This difference affects solubility patterns. Butan-1-ol is more soluble in water than butan-1-amine due to stronger hydrogen bonds.
text_html:
Comparative Solubility with Alcohols
The electronegativity of nitrogen and oxygen are 3.0 and 3.5, respectively. This difference affects solubility patterns. Butan-1-ol is more soluble in water than butan-1-amine due to stronger hydrogen bonds.

subject: Chemistry
text: Intermolecular Associations in Amines - Primary amines show strong hydrogen bonding due to the presence of two hydrogen atoms. - Secondary amines have reduced hydrogen bonding as they contain one hydrogen atom. - Tertiary amines lack intermolecular hydrogen bonding due to the absence of hydrogen atoms.
text_html:
Intermolecular Associations in Amines
- Primary amines show strong hydrogen bonding due to the presence of two hydrogen atoms.
- Secondary amines have reduced hydrogen bonding as they contain one hydrogen atom.
- Tertiary amines lack intermolecular hydrogen bonding due to the absence of hydrogen atoms.

subject: Chemistry
text: Boiling Points of Amines The order of boiling points for isomeric amines is: - Primary > Secondary > Tertiary
text_html:
Boiling Points of Amines
The order of boiling points for isomeric amines is:
- Primary > Secondary > Tertiary

subject: Chemistry
text: Intermolecular Hydrogen Bonding Primary amines engage more in intermolecular association through hydrogen bonding between nitrogen and hydrogen atoms.
text_html:
Intermolecular Hydrogen Bonding
Primary amines engage more in intermolecular association through hydrogen bonding between nitrogen and hydrogen atoms.

subject: Chemistry
text: Boiling Point Comparison Table Here's a snippet from Table 9.2 that compares boiling points: Primary amine (73 molar mass) boils at 350.8 K Secondary amine (73 molar mass) boils at 329.3 K Tertiary amine (73 molar mass) boils at 310.5 K An alkane (72 molar mass) boils at 300.8 K An alcohol (74 molar mass) boils at 390.3 K
text_html:
Boiling Point Comparison Table
Here's a snippet from Table 9.2 that compares boiling points:
  • Primary amine (73 molar mass) boils at 350.8 K
  • Secondary amine (73 molar mass) boils at 329.3 K
  • Tertiary amine (73 molar mass) boils at 310.5 K
  • An alkane (72 molar mass) boils at 300.8 K
  • An alcohol (74 molar mass) boils at 390.3 K

subject: Chemistry
text: Property Recap Combination of key properties: Amines are gases with fishy odor. Primary amines exhibit stronger hydrogen bonds than secondary or tertiary. Amines solubility decreases with an increase in the size of the alkyl group.
text_html:
Property Recap
Combination of key properties:
  • Amines are gases with fishy odor.
  • Primary amines exhibit stronger hydrogen bonds than secondary or tertiary.
  • Amines solubility decreases with an increase in the size of the alkyl group.

subject: Chemistry
text: Reference to Diagram Use Figure 9.2 to see visual representation of intermolecular hydrogen bonding in primary amines.
text_html:
Reference to Diagram
Use Figure 9.2 to see visual representation of intermolecular hydrogen bonding in primary amines.

subject: Chemistry
text: Amines Reactivity Electronegative differences between nitrogen and hydrogen atoms and unshared electron pairs on nitrogen make amines reactive. The type (primary, secondary, tertiary) influences reactions.
text_html:
Amines Reactivity
Electronegative differences between nitrogen and hydrogen atoms and unshared electron pairs on nitrogen make amines reactive. The type (primary, secondary, tertiary) influences reactions.

subject: Chemistry
text: Basic Character of Amines Amines are basic and react with acids to form salts. Regeneration of amines from salts is possible with a base like NaOH.
text_html:
Basic Character of Amines
Amines are basic and react with acids to form salts. Regeneration of amines from salts is possible with a base like NaOH.

subject: Chemistry
text: Structure-Basicity Relationship Amine's basicity ties closely with its structure and ability to stabilize cations formed by protonation. Aliphatic amines are generally stronger bases than ammonia.
text_html:
Structure-Basicity Relationship
Amine's basicity ties closely with its structure and ability to stabilize cations formed by protonation. Aliphatic amines are generally stronger bases than ammonia.

subject: Chemistry
text: Methyl vs. Ethyl Amines The basic strength can vary between methyl and ethyl amines. Factors like solvation and steric hindrance influence basic strength order in aqueous solutions.
text_html:
Methyl vs. Ethyl Amines
The basic strength can vary between methyl and ethyl amines. Factors like solvation and steric hindrance influence basic strength order in aqueous solutions.

subject: Chemistry
text: Arylamines vs. Ammonia Arylamines like aniline are weaker bases due to resonance effects, where the electron pair on nitrogen is less available for protonation.
text_html:
Arylamines vs. Ammonia
Arylamines like aniline are weaker bases due to resonance effects, where the electron pair on nitrogen is less available for protonation.

subject: Chemistry
text: Substituent Effects on Amine Basicity Basic strength in substituted anilines is affected by electron-donating groups (e.g., -OCH 3 , -CH 3 ) increasing basicity and electron-withdrawing groups decreasing it.
text_html:
Substituent Effects on Amine Basicity
Basic strength in substituted anilines is affected by electron-donating groups (e.g., -OCH3, -CH3) increasing basicity and electron-withdrawing groups decreasing it.

subject: Chemistry
text: Alkylation of Amines Amines react with alkyl halides to undergo alkylation, forming different products based on initial amine structure.
text_html:
Alkylation of Amines
Amines react with alkyl halides to undergo alkylation, forming different products based on initial amine structure.

subject: Chemistry
text: Acylation Reaction Primary and secondary amines undergo acylation to form amides. This involves nucleophilic substitution reactions with acid chlorides or anhydrides.
text_html:
Acylation Reaction
Primary and secondary amines undergo acylation to form amides. This involves nucleophilic substitution reactions with acid chlorides or anhydrides.

subject: Chemistry
text: Carbylamine Reaction Primary amines produce isocyanides upon heating with chloroform and ethanolic potassium hydroxide. This reaction is a test for primary amines.
text_html:
Carbylamine Reaction
Primary amines produce isocyanides upon heating with chloroform and ethanolic potassium hydroxide. This reaction is a test for primary amines.

subject: Chemistry
text: Electrophilic Substitution in Aniline Aniline is highly activated due to the resonance effect of -NH 2 , resulting in reactions favoring ortho- and para-positions.
text_html:
Electrophilic Substitution in Aniline
Aniline is highly activated due to the resonance effect of -NH2, resulting in reactions favoring ortho- and para-positions.

subject: Chemistry
text: Definition of Diazonium Salts The general formula for diazonium salts is {tex} R {~N}_2 \overline{{X}} {/tex} . Here, {tex} R {/tex} represents an aryl group, and the ion {tex} \overline{{X}} {/tex} could be {tex} {Cl}^{-} {/tex} , {tex} {Br}^{-} {/tex} , `etc`.
text_html:
Definition of Diazonium Salts
The general formula for diazonium salts is {tex} R {~N}_2 \overline{{X}} {/tex}. Here, {tex} R {/tex} represents an aryl group, and the ion {tex} \overline{{X}} {/tex} could be {tex} {Cl}^{-} {/tex}, {tex} {Br}^{-} {/tex}, `etc`.

subject: Chemistry
text: Naming of Diazonium Salts Diazonium salts are named by adding 'diazonium' to the parent hydrocarbon's name, followed by the anion's name, such as chloride or hydrogensulphate.
text_html:
Naming of Diazonium Salts
Diazonium salts are named by adding 'diazonium' to the parent hydrocarbon's name, followed by the anion's name, such as chloride or hydrogensulphate.

subject: Chemistry
text: Example of Diazonium Salt Benzenediazonium chloride is represented as {tex} {C}_6 {H}_5 \stackrel{+}{{N}}_2 \stackrel{-}{{Cl}} {/tex} , and benzenediazonium hydrogensulphate is given as {tex} {C}_6 {H}_5 {~N}_2^{+} {HSO}_4^{-} {/tex} .
text_html:
Example of Diazonium Salt
Benzenediazonium chloride is represented as {tex} {C}_6 {H}_5 \stackrel{+}{{N}}_2 \stackrel{-}{{Cl}} {/tex}, and benzenediazonium hydrogensulphate is given as {tex} {C}_6 {H}_5 {~N}_2^{+} {HSO}_4^{-} {/tex}.

subject: Chemistry
text: Stability of Diazonium Salts Primary aliphatic amines form unstable alkyldiazonium salts. In contrast, primary aromatic amines form relatively stable arenediazonium salts at low temperatures.
text_html:
Stability of Diazonium Salts
Primary aliphatic amines form unstable alkyldiazonium salts. In contrast, primary aromatic amines form relatively stable arenediazonium salts at low temperatures.

subject: Chemistry
text: Arenediazonium Salt Stability The stability of arenediazonium ions is attributed to resonance effects.
text_html:
Arenediazonium Salt Stability
The stability of arenediazonium ions is attributed to resonance effects.

subject: Chemistry
text: Resonance Concept Resonance is the concept employed to explain the stability of the arenediazonium salts at low temperatures.
text_html:
Resonance Concept
Resonance is the concept employed to explain the stability of the arenediazonium salts at low temperatures.

subject: Chemistry
text: Preparation Method Benzenediazonium chloride is produced by reacting aniline with nitrous acid, generated in-situ by the reaction of sodium nitrite and hydrochloric acid.
text_html:
Preparation Method
Benzenediazonium chloride is produced by reacting aniline with nitrous acid, generated in-situ by the reaction of sodium nitrite and hydrochloric acid.

subject: Chemistry
text: Diazotization Process The conversion of primary aromatic amines to diazonium salts is referred to as diazotization.
text_html:
Diazotization Process
The conversion of primary aromatic amines to diazonium salts is referred to as diazotization.

subject: Chemistry
text: Reaction Equation for Diazotization The reaction formula is: - {tex} {C}_6 {H}_5 {NH}_2+{NaNO}_2+2 {HCl} \xrightarrow{273-278 {~K}} {C}_6 {H}_5 \stackrel{+}{{N}}_2 \stackrel{-}{{Cl}}+{NaCl}+2 {H}_2 {O} {/tex}
text_html:
Reaction Equation for Diazotization
The reaction formula is:
- {tex} {C}_6 {H}_5 {NH}_2+{NaNO}_2+2 {HCl} \xrightarrow{273-278 {~K}} {C}_6 {H}_5 \stackrel{+}{{N}}_2 \stackrel{-}{{Cl}}+{NaCl}+2 {H}_2 {O} {/tex}

subject: Chemistry
text: Characteristics of Diazonium Salts - Diazonium salts are unstable and hence not stored. They are used immediately after production.
text_html:
Characteristics of Diazonium Salts
- Diazonium salts are unstable and hence not stored. They are used immediately after production.

subject: Chemistry
text: Reactions Involving Displacement of Nitrogen Diazonium groups are excellent leaving groups and are replaced by other groups like halides, cyanides, and hydroxyl groups. These replacements lead to nitrogen gas being expelled from the mixture. Halide or cyanide ions: Introduced into the benzene ring using Copper(I) ion, known as the Sandmeyer reaction. Alternatively, chlorine or bromine can be introduced using halogen acid with copper powder, called the Gatterman reaction. Sandmeyer gives a better yield.
text_html:
Reactions Involving Displacement of Nitrogen
Diazonium groups are excellent leaving groups and are replaced by other groups like halides, cyanides, and hydroxyl groups. These replacements lead to nitrogen gas being expelled from the mixture.
  • Halide or cyanide ions: Introduced into the benzene ring using Copper(I) ion, known as the Sandmeyer reaction. Alternatively, chlorine or bromine can be introduced using halogen acid with copper powder, called the Gatterman reaction. Sandmeyer gives a better yield.

subject: Chemistry
text: Replacement by Iodide Ion Direct introduction of iodine into the benzene ring is difficult. However, diazonium salt solution treated with potassium iodide forms iodobenzene.
text_html:
Replacement by Iodide Ion
Direct introduction of iodine into the benzene ring is difficult. However, diazonium salt solution treated with potassium iodide forms iodobenzene.

subject: Chemistry
text: Replacement by Fluoride Ion Treating arenediazonium chloride with fluoroboric acid precipitates arene diazonium fluoroborate which, when heated, decomposes to yield aryl fluoride.
text_html:
Replacement by Fluoride Ion
Treating arenediazonium chloride with fluoroboric acid precipitates arene diazonium fluoroborate which, when heated, decomposes to yield aryl fluoride.

subject: Chemistry
text: Replacement by Hydrogen Mild reducing agents such as hypophosphorous acid or ethanol can reduce diazonium salts to arenes while oxidizing themselves to phosphorous acid and ethanal, respectively.
text_html:
Replacement by Hydrogen
Mild reducing agents such as hypophosphorous acid or ethanol can reduce diazonium salts to arenes while oxidizing themselves to phosphorous acid and ethanal, respectively.

subject: Chemistry
text: Replacement by Hydroxyl Group Allowing the diazonium salt solution to warm up to 283 K causes the salt to hydrolyze into phenol.
text_html:
Replacement by Hydroxyl Group
Allowing the diazonium salt solution to warm up to 283 K causes the salt to hydrolyze into phenol.

subject: Chemistry
text: Replacement by Nitro Group Diazonium fluoroborate heated with aqueous sodium nitrite solution in copper presence replaces the diazonium group with a nitro group.
text_html:
Replacement by Nitro Group
Diazonium fluoroborate heated with aqueous sodium nitrite solution in copper presence replaces the diazonium group with a nitro group.

subject: Chemistry
text: Reactions Involving Retention of Diazo Group Azo products are formed with -N=N- bonds, resulting in compounds with an extended conjugate system. These brightly colored compounds are used as dyes.
text_html:
Reactions Involving Retention of Diazo Group
Azo products are formed with -N=N- bonds, resulting in compounds with an extended conjugate system. These brightly colored compounds are used as dyes.

subject: Chemistry
text: Coupling Reactions Benzene diazonium chloride couples with phenol at the para position to form p-hydroxyazobenzene. Similarly, reactions with aniline yield p-aminoazobenzene — an electrophilic substitution reaction.
text_html:
Coupling Reactions
Benzene diazonium chloride couples with phenol at the para position to form p-hydroxyazobenzene. Similarly, reactions with aniline yield p-aminoazobenzene — an electrophilic substitution reaction.

subject: Chemistry
topic: Importance of Diazonium Salts in Synthetic Organic Chemistry
text: Diazonium Salts as Intermediates Diazonium salts are effective intermediates for introducing various groups into aromatic rings.
text_html:
Diazonium Salts as Intermediates
Diazonium salts are effective intermediates for introducing various groups into aromatic rings.

subject: Chemistry
topic: Importance of Diazonium Salts in Synthetic Organic Chemistry
text: Applications of Diazonium Salts Aryl fluorides and iodides cannot be formed by direct halogenation. Diazonium salts provide a pathway for their formation. The -CN group cannot be directly introduced via nucleophilic substitution in chlorobenzene.
text_html:
Applications of Diazonium Salts
  1. Aryl fluorides and iodides cannot be formed by direct halogenation. Diazonium salts provide a pathway for their formation.
  2. The -CN group cannot be directly introduced via nucleophilic substitution in chlorobenzene.

subject: Chemistry
topic: Importance of Diazonium Salts in Synthetic Organic Chemistry
text: Formation of Cyanobenzene Cyanobenzene is easily synthesized from diazonium salts, unlike through other direct methods.
text_html:
Formation of Cyanobenzene
Cyanobenzene is easily synthesized from diazonium salts, unlike through other direct methods.

subject: Chemistry
topic: Importance of Diazonium Salts in Synthetic Organic Chemistry
text: Substituted Aromatic Compounds Replacement of the diazo group with other groups offers a method to prepare substituted aromatic compounds not achievable by direct substitution in benzene.
text_html:
Substituted Aromatic Compounds
Replacement of the diazo group with other groups offers a method to prepare substituted aromatic compounds not achievable by direct substitution in benzene.

subject: Chemistry
topic: Importance of Diazonium Salts in Synthetic Organic Chemistry
text: Introduction of Diverse Groups Fluoride (-F) Chloride (-Cl) Bromide (-Br) Iodide (-I) Cyano (-CN) Hydroxyl (-OH) Nitro (-NO2) These can be introduced using diazonium salts.
text_html:
Introduction of Diverse Groups
  • Fluoride (-F)
  • Chloride (-Cl)
  • Bromide (-Br)
  • Iodide (-I)
  • Cyano (-CN)
  • Hydroxyl (-OH)
  • Nitro (-NO2)
These can be introduced using diazonium salts.

subject: Chemistry
text: Pharmaceuticals and Medicine Applications of Amines Amines are crucial in synthesizing analgesics like morphine and demerol. Serve as anaesthetics examples include novocaine. Adrenaline and ephedrine, utilized to raise blood pressure, are polyfunctional amines. Antihistamines such as benadryl also contain amine structures.
text_html:
Pharmaceuticals and Medicine Applications of Amines
  • Amines are crucial in synthesizing analgesics like morphine and demerol.
  • Serve as anaesthetics examples include novocaine.
  • Adrenaline and ephedrine, utilized to raise blood pressure, are polyfunctional amines.
  • Antihistamines such as benadryl also contain amine structures.

subject: Chemistry
text: Role of Amines in Neurotransmission Serotonin, significant for regulating hunger, is a biologically active amine. Neurotransmitters with amine groups play vital roles in the nervous system.
text_html:
Role of Amines in Neurotransmission
  • Serotonin, significant for regulating hunger, is a biologically active amine.
  • Neurotransmitters with amine groups play vital roles in the nervous system.

subject: Chemistry
text: Gas Treatment Using Amines Alkanolamines remove CO2 from combustion gases. Sour gases are treated with amines to purify hydrocarbons.
text_html:
Gas Treatment Using Amines
  • Alkanolamines remove CO2 from combustion gases.
  • Sour gases are treated with amines to purify hydrocarbons.

subject: Chemistry
text: Amines in Dye Production Azo dyes, essential to the textile industry, require amines in their production. Used to dye leather, textiles, and nylon.
text_html:
Amines in Dye Production
  • Azo dyes, essential to the textile industry, require amines in their production.
  • Used to dye leather, textiles, and nylon.

subject: Chemistry
text: Corrosion Inhibition Properties of Amines Amines and salts such as morpholine used in boilers to prevent corrosion. Lubricating oils also utilize amines for corrosion protection.
text_html:
Corrosion Inhibition Properties of Amines
  • Amines and salts such as morpholine used in boilers to prevent corrosion.
  • Lubricating oils also utilize amines for corrosion protection.

subject: Chemistry
text: Antioxidant Roles of Diarylamines Enhance life of rubber and roofing asphalt. Employed as stabilizers in cellulose nitrate explosives.
text_html:
Antioxidant Roles of Diarylamines
  • Enhance life of rubber and roofing asphalt.
  • Employed as stabilizers in cellulose nitrate explosives.

subject: Chemistry
text: Surfactants and Softening Agents Quaternary ammonium salts of amines act as surfactants. Useful in fabric softening applications.
text_html:
Surfactants and Softening Agents
  • Quaternary ammonium salts of amines act as surfactants.
  • Useful in fabric softening applications.

subject: Chemistry
text: Amines in Agriculture Manufacture of insecticides and herbicides relies on amines. Utilized in methods for disinfecting drinking water.
text_html:
Amines in Agriculture
  • Manufacture of insecticides and herbicides relies on amines.
  • Utilized in methods for disinfecting drinking water.

subject: Chemistry
text: Photographic Development and Amines Amines are integral components in developing photographic materials.
text_html:
Photographic Development and Amines
  • Amines are integral components in developing photographic materials.

subject: Chemistry
text: Use of Amines in Everyday Products Incorporated in the production of soaps, creams, and shampoos. Act as solvents in creating plastics and polymers. Vital components in proteins and various vitamins.
text_html:
Use of Amines in Everyday Products
  • Incorporated in the production of soaps, creams, and shampoos.
  • Act as solvents in creating plastics and polymers.
  • Vital components in proteins and various vitamins.
